hypotheses.title: Serpentinization-Condition Fenton Chemistry Generates the Same Specific PLOOHs Found in Ferroptosis
hypotheses.bridge_type: Fe(II)/Fe(III) Fenton cycling kinetics + PLOOH intermediate chemistry
hypotheses.key_prediction: AA-PE vesicles under serpentinization Fenton conditions produce PE-AA-OOH with same positional isomer distribution as non-enzymatic ferroptotic PLOOHs

hypotheses.title: The Labile Iron Pool Set-Point (~0.5-1 uM) Reflects the Fenton-PLOOH/GPX4 Kinetic Crossover Calibrated Against Ancestral Serpentinization Fe(II)
hypotheses.key_prediction: Reconstituted GPX4-vesicle system shows critical [Fe(II)] threshold for runaway PLOOH accumulation at 0.3-3 uM, matching biological LIP

hypotheses.title: Ferrihydrite Mineral Surfaces Exhibit Partial Positional Selectivity in PUFA Oxidation -- A Mineral Proto-Lipoxygenase
hypotheses.key_prediction: Ferrihydrite-catalyzed AA oxidation shows >1.5-fold enrichment of specific positional isomers vs homogeneous Fenton controls

hypotheses.title: Prebiotic Ferroptosis -- Ferrihydrite Fenton Destruction of Unsaturated Vesicles as Selection Pressure for Saturated Membranes
hypotheses.bridge_type: Ferrihydrite surface-catalyzed lipid peroxidation + Evolutionary selection
hypotheses.key_prediction: Mixed unsaturated/saturated vesicle populations show >5-fold differential survival under ferrihydrite Fenton conditions

hypotheses.title: GPX4 Selenocysteine Catalysis Recapitulates Abiotic Se-Mineral PLOOH Reduction from Serpentinization
hypotheses.key_prediction: Na2Se (10-100 uM) reduces Fenton-mediated PLOOH accumulation by >50% in model vesicles at serpentinization conditions

hypotheses.title: ER as Intracellular Serpentinization Redox Interface -- Fe(II)/H2O2 Overlap Determines Ferroptosis Initiation Site
hypotheses.key_prediction: Ero1alpha inhibitor EN460 delays ferroptosis onset >2-fold by disrupting the Fe(II)/H2O2 overlap at ER membranes

hypotheses.title: Fenton Peroxidation of Prebiotic Fatty Acid Vesicles Induces Phase Separation for Primitive Compartmentalization
hypotheses.bridge_type: Membrane phase behavior under Fenton peroxidation
hypotheses.key_prediction: >3-fold increase in Laurdan GP heterogeneity in mixed fatty acid vesicles under ferrihydrite Fenton conditions

bridge_mechanisms_used: Membrane phase behavior under Fenton peroxidation
self_critique_issues: Two citations downgraded GROUNDED->PARAMETRIC: Michel ferrihydrite structure (~2007-2010), GPX4 rate constant (~10^8 M-1s-1)
PE ester hydrolysis at 200C/pH 10 threatens H1 feasibility -- mitigated by free-AA control in protocol
Geometric matching argument in H3 is physically weak at 60C thermal energy -- confidence set to 3
Se availability in serpentinization fluids (H5) is poorly constrained -- acknowledged in counter-evidence
Scale mismatch in H6 (geochemical hundreds of meters vs intracellular micrometers) weakens analogy
Fatty acid vesicle monomer exchange dynamics may undermine H7 domain stability

constraint_compliance.named_molecular_species: H1: PE-AA-OOH, PE-AdA-OOH; H3: ferrihydrite, arachidonic acid; H5: FeSe2, selenocysteine
constraint_compliance.gpx4_abiotic_equivalent_check: PASS -- no hypothesis claims GPX4 equivalent in abiotic system; H5 frames mineral Se as evolutionary precursor
constraint_compliance.geological_timescale_check: PASS -- all experiments use lab-scale (37-200C, hours-days)
